message:
Add SQLite storage layer scaffolding

Establish storage module structure with placeholder sqlite.rs for
future rusqlite-based persistence implementation.

Module purpose (per PLAN § 3.1, § 4):
The storage layer will encapsulate all SQLite database operations for
persisting normalized conversation data extracted by connectors. This
includes schema initialization, CRUD operations on the seven-table
model (agents, workspaces, conversations, messages, snippets, tags,
conversation_tags), and FTS5 virtual table maintenance for fallback
text search when Tantivy is unavailable or disabled.

Planned sqlite.rs responsibilities:
- Database initialization: CREATE TABLE statements per PLAN § 4.2
  schema with appropriate indexes (conversations.agent_id/started_at,
  messages.conversation_id/idx, messages.created_at), foreign key
  constraints (ON DELETE CASCADE for messages/snippets when parent
  conversation deleted), and performance pragmas (journal_mode=WAL for
  concurrent readers during indexing, synchronous=NORMAL for faster
  writes, temp_store=MEMORY, cache_size=-65536 for 64MB page cache,
  mmap_size=268435456 for 256MB memory-mapped I/O)

- Schema migrations: Version tracking in meta table with Rust-implemented
  up/down migration functions (not SQL files) to handle future schema
  evolution, rebuild Tantivy index when schema incompatible

- Conversation upsert operations: Insert or update conversations based
  on (agent_id, external_id) UNIQUE constraint to enable idempotent
  connector rescans—if source log unchanged, skip re-parsing; if
  modified, update ended_at and replace messages

- Message batch insertion: Transactional INSERTs for messages within
  conversation using prepared statements and explicit BEGIN/COMMIT for
  bulk import performance during initial scan (target >10k messages/sec
  per PLAN § 12.3 performance baseline)

- FTS5 virtual table sync: Populate fts_messages virtual table
  (content, title, agent_slug, workspace, message_id, conversation_id,
  created_at columns per PLAN § 4.4) from messages table rows in same
  transaction as conversation insert to maintain consistency—avoids
  SQLite triggers for deterministic timing

- Query interfaces: Fetch conversation by id with eager-loaded messages
  and snippets (single query via JOIN), list conversations filtered by
  agent/workspace/time range for UI result display, search fts_messages
  with MATCH operator for fallback text search when Tantivy disabled

Rusqlite configuration (per Cargo.toml):
- bundled feature: Compile libsqlite3 from source and statically link,
  ensuring FTS5 extension availability across all platforms (some distro
  SQLite packages disable FTS5) and eliminating runtime dependency on
  system libsqlite3.so
- modern_sqlite feature: Enable recent SQLite compile-time options
  like SQLITE_ENABLE_FTS5, SQLITE_ENABLE_JSON1 (for JSON_EXTRACT in
  metadata queries), SQLITE_ENABLE_RTREE (not used but included),
  SQLITE_DQS=0 (strict quote handling)

Connection pooling strategy:
Single Connection wrapped in Arc<Mutex<Connection>> for exclusive write
access from indexer thread, no need for connection pool (r2d2/deadpool)
since reads primarily come from Tantivy index. SQLite WAL mode allows
concurrent readers via separate read-only Connections if needed for
export/inspect subcommands while indexer is running.

Error handling approach:
rusqlite errors wrapped in anyhow::Context for human-friendly error
messages (e.g., "failed to insert conversation for codex session XYZ:
UNIQUE constraint failed" rather than raw SQLite error codes). Critical
errors like schema initialization failures should panic with descriptive
messages at startup; runtime query errors should propagate to caller
for display in UI.

This commit contains only the module declaration (mod.rs) and empty
placeholder file (sqlite.rs) to establish the storage/ namespace per
implementation checklist (PLAN § 14.2: core modules). Actual schema
definition, migration system, and query functions will be implemented
in subsequent commits after TUI mockup demonstrates end-to-end data flow
(PLAN § 14: "minimal TUI" with dummy data precedes "full `index`
command" to validate UI requirements drive storage API design).

Related: PLAN_TO_MAKE_CODING_AGENT_SESSION_SEARCH.md § 3.1 (storage
component), § 4.2 (SQLite schema), § 4.3 (performance tuning), § 4.4
(FTS5 mirror), Cargo.toml (rusqlite bundled + modern_sqlite features),
README.md (storage/ module description).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -0,0 +1 @@
+//! SQLite backend placeholder; schema and migrations live here.
